stem: Simplify \( \dfrac{\sin(2\theta)}{2\sin\theta} \) using an identity, then evaluate it at \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[d]]} \).
solution: By the double-angle identity \( \sin 2\theta = 2\sin\theta\cos\theta \), so \( \dfrac{\sin 2\theta}{2\sin\theta} = \cos\theta \). Evaluating at \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[d]]} \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This cancels incorrectly to \( \sin\theta \) rather than \( \cos\theta \).
This confuses the simplified form with \( \tan\theta \).
This forgets to cancel the factor of \( 2 \), leaving \( 2\cos\theta \).
This has the wrong sign.
This leaves the numerator as \( \cos 2\theta \) instead of simplifying the whole ratio.
This fails to divide by \( 2\sin\theta \), reporting \( \sin 2\theta \).

stem: Simplify \( \dfrac{\sin^2\theta}{1-\cos\theta} \) using the Pythagorean identity, then evaluate it at \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[d]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This keeps the original denominator instead of the simplified \( 1+\cos\theta \).
This drops the leading \( 1 \) and reports only \( \cos\theta \).
This confuses the simplified expression with \( \sin\theta \).
This has the wrong sign on the constant term.
This adds \( \sin\theta \) instead of \( \cos\theta \).
This has the wrong sign overall.

stem: Simplify \( \dfrac{1-\cos(2\theta)}{1+\cos(2\theta)} \) using a double-angle identity, then evaluate it at \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[d]]} \).
solution: Since \( 1-\cos 2\theta = 2\sin^2\theta \) and \( 1+\cos 2\theta = 2\cos^2\theta \), the ratio simplifies to \( \tan^2\theta \). Evaluating at \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[d]]} \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to square \( \tan\theta \).
This has the wrong sign on the squared tangent.
This inverts the simplified expression, giving \( \cot^2\theta \) with the wrong sign.
This flips the numerator and denominator, giving \( \cot^2\theta \) instead of \( \tan^2\theta \).
This leaves the answer in terms of \( \cos 2\theta \) instead of simplifying fully.
This adds \( 1 \) to the correct simplified value.

stem: Simplify \( \dfrac{1-\cos\theta}{\sin\theta} \) using a half-angle identity, then evaluate it at \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[d]]} \).
solution: This ratio is the half-angle identity for tangent: \( \dfrac{1-\cos\theta}{\sin\theta} = \tan\dfrac{\theta}{2} \). Evaluating at \( \theta = \dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[d]]} \) gives \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the reciprocal of the correct ratio.
This confuses the half-angle tangent identity with a Pythagorean-style sum.
This computes \( \cot\theta \) instead of \( \tan\tfrac{\theta}{2} \).
This reports \( \sin\theta \) alone, ignoring the identity.
This has the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct value.
